2603.09931.title: Adaptive Clinical-Aware Latent Diffusion for Multimodal Brain Image Generation and Missing Modality Imputation
2603.09931.paper_summary: Multimodal neuroimaging provides complementary insights for Alzheimer's disease diagnosis, yet clinical datasets frequently suffer from missing modalities. We propose ACADiff, a framework that synthesizes missing brain imaging modalities through adaptive clinical-aware diffusion. ACADiff learns mappings between incomplete multimodal observations and target modalities by progressively denoising latent representations while attending to available imaging data and clinical metadata. The framework employs adaptive fusion that dynamically reconfigures based on input availability, coupled with semantic clinical guidance via GPT-4o-encoded prompts. Three specialized generators enable bidirectional synthesis among sMRI, FDG-PET, and AV45-PET. Evaluated on ADNI subjects, ACADiff achieves superior generation quality and maintains robust diagnostic performance even under extreme 80\% missing scenarios, outperforming all existing baselines. To promote reproducibility, code is available at https://github.com/rongzhou7/ACADiff
2603.09931.paper_summary_zh: 多模態神經影像學為阿茲海默症的診斷提供了互補的見解，但臨床數據集經常面臨缺少模態的問題。我們提出了 ACADiff，一個通過自適應臨床感知擴散合成缺失腦部影像模態的框架。ACADiff 通過逐步去噪潛在表示來學習不完整多模態觀察與目標模態之間的映射，同時關注可用的影像數據和臨床元數據。該框架採用自適應融合，根據輸入可用性動態重新配置，並通過 GPT-4o 編碼提示提供語義臨床指導。三個專門的生成器使得 sMRI、FDG-PET 和 AV45-PET 之間的雙向合成成為可能。在 ADNI 受試者上進行評估時，ACADiff 實現了卓越的生成質量，並在極端的 80\% 缺失情況下仍維持穩健的診斷性能，超越了所有現有基準。為了促進可重複性，代碼可在 https://github.com/rongzhou7/ACADiff 獲得。
2603.09931.author: Rong Zhou et.al.
2603.09931.authors: Rong Zhou, Houliang Zhou, Yao Su, Brian Y. Chen, Yu Zhang, Lifang He, Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative
